title: Climate Change Risk Perception and Policy Preferences: The Role of Affect, Imagery, and Values

tax_mentions.context: Support/opposition item to encourage less driving and reduce carbon dioxide emissions.
Support/opposition item to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by raising business energy prices.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Cap-and-trade/emissions trading, not a direct tax.

inclusion_reason: The survey directly measured support/opposition for a 60-cent per gallon gasoline tax to reduce CO2 emissions, providing direct tax-acceptability evidence.
